identity.meaning: Represents the rising sun and aggressive cricketing identity

ownership_history: Formed after termination of Deccan Chargers in 2012 by IPL governing council
Sun TV Network acquired the franchise and rebranded it as Sunrisers Hyderabad

history.full_text: Sunrisers Hyderabad (SRH) is a franchise cricket team based in Hyderabad, Telangana, India, that competes in the Indian Premier League (IPL). The team was established in 2013 after the Deccan Chargers franchise was terminated due to financial issues. The Sun Group, a major media conglomerate, acquired the franchise and introduced Sunrisers Hyderabad as a new entity. From the beginning, SRH built a reputation as a team heavily reliant on disciplined bowling attacks. Over the years, the team evolved significantly, transitioning into a high-powered batting unit while still maintaining its bowling strength. SRH achieved its greatest success in 2016 when it won the IPL title under the captaincy of David Warner. The team consistently remained competitive between 2016 and 2018, reaching multiple playoffs and finals. However, the franchise experienced a dip in performance between 2021 and 2023. In recent seasons, SRH has re-emerged as an aggressive batting side, setting multiple high-scoring records including one of the highest team totals in IPL history.
timeline: 2013: Franchise established after Deccan Chargers termination
2016: Won first IPL title under David Warner
2018: Reached IPL final under Kane Williamson
2024: Return to finals with aggressive batting approach
2025–present: Focus on explosive batting and new leadership

key_eras.description: Golden phase with strong bowling attack and consistent playoff appearances including a title and finals
Transitional phase with competitive but inconsistent performances
Decline phase marked by poor team balance and leadership instability
Revival phase characterized by aggressive batting strategy and record-breaking totals

season_highlights.note: High scoring matches and batting dominance

weaknesses: Frequent leadership changes during slump years
records: One of the highest team totals in IPL history (287 runs)
Consistently strong performance in defending totals during peak years

rivalries.details: Major rivalry highlighted by the IPL 2016 final where SRH defeated RCB
Recent rivalry intensified due to playoff and final clashes
